Apple___Apple_scab: Apply fungicides like Mancozeb regularly. Remove fallen leaves to reduce spores.
Apple___Black_rot: Prune infected branches, use copper-based fungicides, and ensure good air circulation.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust: Use resistant apple varieties, remove nearby cedar trees, and apply fungicides.
Apple___healthy: No action needed, the apple plant is healthy.
Blueberry___healthy: No disease detected. Maintain regular watering and soil health.
Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew: Spray sulfur fungicides and improve air circulation around trees.
Cherry_(including_sour)___healthy: No disease detected. Keep up good care.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray: Apply fungicides like Azoxystrobin, rotate crops to prevent disease buildup.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust: Use resistant varieties and fungicide applications.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight: Apply fungicide, remove debris, and practice crop rotation.
Corn_(maize)___healthy: Plant looks healthy. Continue proper farming practices.
Grape___Black_rot: Apply sulfur or copper fungicides regularly and remove infected parts.
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles): Remove infected wood and avoid overhead irrigation.
Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo: Spray appropriate fungicides and ensure good ventilation.
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening): Use disease-free seedlings, control psyllid vectors, and remove infected trees.
Peach___Bacterial_spot: Apply copper-based sprays and and remove infected fruits/leaves.
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot: Use disease-free seeds, copper sprays, and maintain good sanitation.
Potato___Early_blight: Apply fungicides regularly and avoid overhead irrigation.
Potato___Late_blight: Use fungicides like Metalaxyl and remove infected leaves.
Soybean___healthy: Healthy crop, continue good farming practices.
Squash___Powdery_mildew: Spray potassium bicarbonate or sulfur fungicides regularly.
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch: Use resistant varieties and remove infected leaves.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot: Apply copper sprays and remove infected leaves.
Tomato___Early_blight: Use fungicides like Chlorothalonil and crop rotation.
Tomato___Late_blight: Apply systemic fungicides and remove infected plants.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot: Use fungicides and remove infected debris.
Tomato___Target_Spot: Apply fungicides and maintain proper spacing.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus: Use virus-free seeds and control whitefly vectors.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus: Use virus-resistant varieties and sanitize tools.
